 {"id":"bd-e166","title":"Improve timestamp comparison readability in import","description":"The timestamp comparison logic uses double-negative which can be confusing:

Current code:
if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // skip update
}

More readable:
if incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // perform update
} else {
    // skip (local is newer)
}

This is a minor refactor for code clarity.

Related: bd-1022
Files: internal/importer/importer.go:411, 488","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T15:32:12.27108-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T22:25:27.124071-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T22:25:27.124071-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e6x","title":"bd sync --squash: batch multiple syncs into single commit","description":"For solo developers who don't need real-time multi-agent coordination, add a --squash option to bd sync that accumulates changes and commits them in a single commit rather than one commit per sync.

This addresses the git history pollution concern (many 'bd sync: timestamp' commits) while preserving the default behavior needed for orchestration.

**Proposed behavior:**
- `bd sync --squash` accumulates pending exports without committing
- Commits accumulated changes on session end or explicit `bd sync` (without --squash)
- Default behavior unchanged (immediate commits for orchestration)

**Use case:** Solo developers who want cleaner git history but don't need real-time coordination between agents.

**Related:** PR #411 (docs: reduce bd sync commit pollution)
**See also:** Multi-repo support as alternative solution (docs/MULTI_REPO_AGENTS.md)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-28T18:21:47.789887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.608286-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:56:57.608777-08:00"}

-{"id":"bd-emg","title":"bd init should refuse when JSONL already has issues (safety guard)","description":"When running `bd init` in a directory with an existing JSONL containing issues, bd should refuse and suggest the correct action instead of proceeding.

## The Problem

Current behavior when database is missing but JSONL exists:
```
$ bd create "test"
Error: no beads database found
Hint: run 'bd init' to create a database...
```

This leads users (and AI agents) to reflexively run `bd init`, which can cause:
- Prefix mismatch if wrong prefix specified
- Data corruption if JSONL is damaged
- Confusion about what actually happened

## Proposed Behavior

```
$ bd init --prefix bd

⚠ Found existing .beads/issues.jsonl with 76 issues.

This appears to be a fresh clone, not a new project.

To hydrate the database from existing JSONL:
  bd doctor --fix

To force re-initialization (may cause data loss):
  bd init --prefix bd --force

Aborting.
```

## Trigger Conditions

- `.beads/issues.jsonl` or `.beads/beads.jsonl` exists
- File contains > 0 valid issue lines
- No `--force` flag provided

## Edge Cases

- Empty JSONL (0 issues) → allow init (new project)
- Corrupted JSONL → warn but allow with confirmation
- Existing `.db` file → definitely refuse (weird state)

## Related

- bd-dmb: Fresh clone should suggest hydration (better error messages)
- bd-4ew: bd doctor should detect fresh clone

This issue is about the safety guard on `bd init` itself, not the error messages from other commands.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-28T18:21:41.149304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T18:21:41.149304-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-emg","title":"bd init should refuse when JSONL already has issues (safety guard)","description":"When running `bd init` in a directory with an existing JSONL containing issues, bd should refuse and suggest the correct action instead of proceeding.

## The Problem

Current behavior when database is missing but JSONL exists:
```
$ bd create "test"
Error: no beads database found
Hint: run 'bd init' to create a database...
```

This leads users (and AI agents) to reflexively run `bd init`, which can cause:
- Prefix mismatch if wrong prefix specified
- Data corruption if JSONL is damaged
- Confusion about what actually happened

## Proposed Behavior

```
$ bd init --prefix bd

⚠ Found existing .beads/issues.jsonl with 76 issues.

This appears to be a fresh clone, not a new project.

To hydrate the database from existing JSONL:
  bd doctor --fix

To force re-initialization (may cause data loss):
  bd init --prefix bd --force

Aborting.
```

## Trigger Conditions

- `.beads/issues.jsonl` or `.beads/beads.jsonl` exists
- File contains > 0 valid issue lines
- No `--force` flag provided

## Edge Cases

- Empty JSONL (0 issues) → allow init (new project)
- Corrupted JSONL → warn but allow with confirmation
- Existing `.db` file → definitely refuse (weird state)

## Related

- bd-dmb: Fresh clone should suggest hydration (better error messages)
- bd-4ew: bd doctor should detect fresh clone

This issue is about the safety guard on `bd init` itself, not the error messages from other commands.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-28T18:21:41.149304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:18.849507-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:18.849507-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-f0n","title":"Git history fallback missing timeout - could hang on large repos","description":"## Problem

The git commands in `checkGitHistoryForDeletions` have no timeout. On large repos with extensive history, `git log --all -S` or `git log --all -G` can take a very long time (minutes).

## Location
`internal/importer/importer.go:899` and `:930`

## Impact
- Import could hang indefinitely
- User has no feedback that git search is running
- No way to cancel except killing the process

## Fix
Add context with timeout to git commands:

```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
defer cancel()
cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", ...)
```

Also consider adding a `--since` flag to bound the git history search.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:48:24.388639-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:53.669714-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:53.669714-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ge7","title":"Improve Beads test coverage from 46% to 80%","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-20T21:21:03.700271-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.608871-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:56:04.085939-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ghb","title":"Add --yes flag to bd doctor --fix for non-interactive mode","description":"## Feature Request

Add a `--yes` or `-y` flag to `bd doctor --fix` that automatically confirms all prompts, enabling non-interactive usage in scripts and CI/CD pipelines.

## Current Behavior
`bd doctor --fix` prompts for confirmation before applying fixes, which blocks automated workflows.

## Desired Behavior
`bd doctor --fix --yes` should apply all fixes without prompting.

## Use Cases
- CI/CD pipelines that need to auto-fix issues
- Scripts that automate repository setup
- Pre-commit hooks that want to silently fix issues","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:22:45.486584-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.609134-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:55:06.895066-08:00"}
